message:
fix(signal): use saturating_add(1) to preserve multiple delivery count

Fix Signal::recv() to increment seen_deliveries by 1 instead of
jumping to the current delivery count. The previous behavior
(self.seen_deliveries = current) would skip intermediate deliveries
when multiple signals arrived between polls — e.g., if 3 signals
were delivered, the first recv() would consume all 3 at once by
jumping the counter, making subsequent recv() calls see no pending
deliveries.

Now uses saturating_add(1) so each recv() consumes exactly one
delivery, allowing callers to observe every signal individually.

Includes a new test (signal_recv_preserves_multiple_recorded_deliveries)
that injects two signals before polling and verifies both are received
as separate recv() calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
